filled_slots.reported_issue: Vendor information is not visible in SENTRY

assumptions: The Vendor Directory is loading but receiving zero visible vendors rather than failing completely.
The canonical profile CSV in 00_System is present and should be used to bootstrap directory visibility when needed.
A backend restart is acceptable to activate startup bootstrap logic.
A cached frontend query failure may be preventing vendor data from repopulating after the backend recovered.
A cached successful-empty response may also be preventing the default Vendor Directory view from self-healing.
The backend should prefer source-backed vendor profiles from Desktop Vendor Assessments over DB-derived visibility when the source is available.
The broader Desktop SENTRY workspace is now the explicit user-designated local data root for incident, project, regulatory, and vendor source material.
open_questions: Whether the current DB is actually empty versus populated with only noncanonical vendor rows.
Whether any separate frontend cache or browser state is also obscuring the refreshed directory after backend restart.
Whether the user has refreshed the browser tab since the cached-error retry guard was added.
Whether the browser is currently pointed at the active Vite instance if multiple localhost ports were used during recovery.
Whether the backend process has been restarted since source-backed directory preference was added.
Whether uvicorn reload mode is leaving the backend unavailable after filesystem-triggered restarts inside backend\.venv.
Whether the user has restarted the backend using the new stable launcher behavior.
Whether the frontend has reloaded the new localhost same-origin API routing logic.
Whether stale Vite processes on port 3000 were causing the user to browse an outdated frontend instance.
Whether the backend launcher was failing before bind because uv was unavailable on the local machine.
